primaryGoal: Immediate emotional dominance and protection of child
commonTacticsUsed: Emotional flooding; character attacks; district threats; repeated accusations
escalationTriggers: Interruption; policy jargon; perceived dismissal; defending teacher too quickly
calmingFactors: Uninterrupted venting; calm tone; emotional validation; structured next steps
hiddenFearVulnerability: Loss of control / child being mistreated
typicalPhrases: This is ridiculous. / Nobody listens. / I’m not letting this go.
likelyEndingState: Emotionally exhausted but calmer if contained properly

primaryGoal: Reduce or avoid consequence
commonTacticsUsed: Forced alliance; minimization; bargaining; boys will be boys
escalationTriggers: Rigid language; inability to negotiate; embarrassment themes
calmingFactors: Flexibility where possible; collaborative language; dignity preservation
typicalPhrases: Come on, kids make mistakes. / There has to be another way.
likelyEndingState: Attempts compromise until clear boundary holds

primaryGoal: Protect child emotionally
commonTacticsUsed: Guilt appeals; emotional storytelling; alliance recruitment; victim expansion
escalationTriggers: Perceived coldness; lack of empathy; policy-first responses
calmingFactors: Warmth; listening; emotional acknowledgment; reassurance child is cared for
typicalPhrases: She’s been through so much already. / You don’t understand how sensitive she is.
likelyEndingState: Can calm significantly once emotionally heard

primaryGoal: Challenge school authority/process
commonTacticsUsed: Policy attacks; documentation demands; implied legal threats; procedural traps
calmingFactors: Precision; professionalism; documentation; calm structure
typicalPhrases: I’d like this documented. / What policy are you using?
manipulationStyle: Pressure through procedure and implied consequences
likelyEndingState: Respects calm competence

primaryGoal: Prove long-term mistreatment pattern
commonTacticsUsed: Stacking grievances; historical expansion; generalized accusations
escalationTriggers: Minimizing past concerns; rushing conversation; dismissing patterns
calmingFactors: Acknowledgment of history; narrowing focus calmly
typicalPhrases: This has been happening for years. / Nobody ever listens to us.
likelyEndingState: May continue circling unless anchored carefully

primaryGoal: Force compliance through pressure
commonTacticsUsed: Volume escalation; insults; authority challenges; intimidation
calmingFactors: Calm firmness; confident pacing; emotional non-reactivity
typicalPhrases: You people are unbelievable. / Do you know who I am?
likelyEndingState: Backs down when structure holds consistently

primaryGoal: Recruit educator emotionally
commonTacticsUsed: Flattery; alliance-building; subtle minimization
calmingFactors: Friendly professionalism; calm empathy
typicalPhrases: I know you’re one of the good ones. / You understand kids.
likelyEndingState: May pivot to frustration if charm fails

primaryGoal: End conversation quickly
commonTacticsUsed: Avoidance; minimal answers; disengagement
calmingFactors: Simple clarity; nonjudgmental tone
typicalPhrases: Okay. / I guess. / Whatever you need to do.
likelyEndingState: Often emotionally unresolved after call

primaryGoal: Protect child from perceived harm
commonTacticsUsed: Victim expansion; staff distrust; emotional flooding
escalationTriggers: Questioning child credibility; minimizing child fear
calmingFactors: Safety-focused language; reassurance; calm listening
hiddenFearVulnerability: Child being unsafe or emotionally harmed
typicalPhrases: My child is not lying. / Something is clearly wrong here.
likelyEndingState: Can become collaborative once safety is established

emotionalStyle: Angry at the system rather than just incident
primaryGoal: Expose institutional unfairness
commonTacticsUsed: Generalized attacks; district complaints; system distrust
calmingFactors: Human acknowledgment; authenticity; transparency
typicalPhrases: This district never cares about families. / You’re all covering for each other.
likelyEndingState: May calm if conversation feels genuinely human

primaryGoal: Protect family identity and normalize behavior
commonTacticsUsed: Minimization; forced alliance; humor; storytelling
calmingFactors: Respectful accountability; strength-based framing
hiddenFearVulnerability: Child being labeled bad like they once were
typicalPhrases: I was the same way at that age. / Boys are boys. / I got in trouble all the time too.
relationshipToSchool: May distrust schools but respects toughness
likelyEndingState: Can accept consequences if dignity and respect remain intact

emotionalStyle: Defensive on surface but privately impressed
primaryGoal: Appear responsible while internally admiring child’s boldness
commonTacticsUsed: Minimization; subtle bragging; emotional reframing
escalationTriggers: Public embarrassment; disrespect toward family identity
calmingFactors: Humor; calm professionalism; focus on future consequences
typicalPhrases: Well… I’m not saying it was right, BUT… / Honestly that sounds like something his grandfather would’ve done.
likelyEndingState: Often becomes cooperative if not shamed

emotionalStyle: Hardened, emotionally resilient, distrustful of weakness
primaryGoal: Prepare child for real world toughness
commonTacticsUsed: Minimization of emotional harm; comparison to harsher upbringing
escalationTriggers: Language implying child is fragile or damaged
calmingFactors: Respectful realism; accountability tied to growth
typicalPhrases: Nobody babied me growing up. / Life’s harder than this.
relationshipToSchool: Respects strong leadership more than warmth
likelyEndingState: Responds best to calm competence and directness

emotionalStyle: Polite on the surface but emotionally loaded
primaryGoal: Apply pressure while preserving plausible deniability
commonTacticsUsed: Subtle threats; guilt; sarcasm; implied criticism
calmingFactors: Calm professionalism; direct clarity; documented next steps
hiddenFearVulnerability: Means of maintaining control without open conflict
typicalPhrases: I’m just trying to understand… / Interesting that this keeps happening. / I’ll make a few calls if needed.
likelyEndingState: Often escalates quietly if not handled clearly

primaryGoal: Protect child from consequences through status leverage
commonTacticsUsed: Name-dropping; authority references; special treatment expectations
calmingFactors: Confident professionalism; consistency; calm boundaries
hiddenFearVulnerability: Child losing status, privilege, or image
typicalPhrases: I know the superintendent. / This will not be handled like a normal situation. / Do you know who my family is?
likelyEndingState: Respects leaders who remain calm and unmoved by status

primaryGoal: Protect child identity from blame
commonTacticsUsed: Reality denial; staff distrust; evidence reframing
calmingFactors: Patient grounding; specific facts; calm pacing
hiddenFearVulnerability: Child being bad or morally flawed
typicalPhrases: My child would never do that. / That doesn’t even sound like him. / Someone else is lying.
likelyEndingState: May slowly shift if facts remain calm and consistent

primaryGoal: Avoid consequences viewed as harmful or negative
commonTacticsUsed: Emotional reframing; anti-discipline philosophy; over-processing
calmingFactors: Collaborative language; growth framing; restorative language
typicalPhrases: I don’t believe punishment teaches anything. / Children act out because needs aren’t met. / Can’t we handle this another way?
likelyEndingState: Responds best to restorative and growth-centered framing

primaryGoal: Wants the school to fix what they can’t control at home
commonTacticsUsed: Avoidance; emotional surrender; inconsistency
escalationTriggers: Being asked to enforce difficult accountability at home
calmingFactors: Clear structure; manageable next steps; calm support
typicalPhrases: I don’t know what else to do with him. / Maybe he listens to you more than me. / I’ve tried everything.
manipulationStyle: Withdrawal and dependency on school support
likelyEndingState: Often relieved when leadership feels stable and competent

primaryGoal: Pressure the school through visibility and public outrage
commonTacticsUsed: Recording threats; Facebook/community-group threats; public framing
calmingFactors: Transparency; calm documentation; direct communication
hiddenFearVulnerability: Fear of being ignored or powerless
typicalPhrases: I’ll post this everywhere if I have to. / People need to know how this school treats kids. / I already told other parents.
likelyEndingState: May calm if they feel publicly respected and heard

primaryGoal: Maintain closeness and approval from child
commonTacticsUsed: Minimization; humor; anti-authority framing
calmingFactors: Respectful realism; future-oriented accountability
typicalPhrases: Kids are kids. / I’d probably have done the same thing. / He’s just having fun.
likelyEndingState: Responds best when accountability is framed as growth rather than punishment
